results.title: Principal, Architecture – AI, Unified Commerce at Gap Inc. - United States Remote
highlights:
Principal, Architecture – AI, Unified Commerce at Gap Inc. - United States Remote
...
# Principal, Architecture – AI, Unified Commerce
...
🕒 June 5
...
🏄 California – Remote🏄 California – Remote
...
⏰ Full Time
...
🤖 Artificial Intelligence
...
### 📋 Description
...
• Drive the vision, design, and execution of an integrated technology architecture that powers seamless, intelligent, and personalized customer experiences across all digital and physical touchpoints. • Champion the use of Agentic Commerce patterns—autonomous shopping agents, proactive assistant bots, and AI-driven fulfillment orchestration—to create frictionless, next-gen shopping experiences. • Design intelligent, AI-augmented experiences that integrate predictive personalization, dynamic pricing, virtual styling, conversational commerce, and autonomous checkout journeys. • Integrate commerce ecosystems with real-time decision engines, customer data platforms (CDPs), recommendation systems, and AI models to drive adaptive journeys. • Partner with product, engineering, and data science teams to implement AI across the customer lifecycle—from acquisition and engagement to loyalty and retention. • Define architectural principles to scale AI/ML use cases, leveraging cloud-native platforms (GCP, AWS), serverless models, and data mesh strategies. • Architect future-ready, omnichannel commerce platforms supporting unified experiences such as BOPIS, Same-day Delivery, and Endless Aisle. • Ensure architecture enables real-time signals processing (e.g., clickstream, IoT, POS), contextual understanding, and action-triggering across commerce surfaces. • Lead architectural reviews and governance for AI-based and connected commerce projects. • Guide experimentation with GenAI, LLMs, and autonomous agents in commerce, marketing, and fulfillment use cases. • Collaborate with data, privacy, and compliance teams to align AI-powered experiences with data governance and ethical AI frameworks.
...
### 🎯 Requirements
...
• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or related field; Master’s preferred. • 10+ years of architecture, with at least 5+ years in commerce or omnichannel large scale transformation roles. • Demonstrated experience architecting and delivering commerce platforms across: Headless eCommerce (Shopify, Salesforce Commerce, etc.) • Digital Experience Platforms (CMS, DAM, PIM) • Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), Loyalty & Personalization Engines • Retail POS, OMS, and Fulfillment Systems • Deep knowledge of cloud-native architectures, microservices, event-driven systems (Kafka, GCP Pub/Sub), and serverless patterns. • Experience integrating AI/ML services into customer-facing applications, preferably in real-time or large-scale environments • Experience with Agentic AI frameworks or commerce focused LLMs. • Familiarity with AI platforms like Vertex AI, Amazon Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, or similar. • Working knowledge of data pipelines, model deployment (MLOps), and experimentation platforms. • Exposure to composable commerce and MACH (Microservices, API-first, Cloud-native, Headless) architecture. • Retail, CPG, or DTC brand experience with personalization and omnichannel engagement initiative • Strategic mindset with a strong grasp of emerging tech and its impact on business. • Excellent communication and storytelling skills—especially around complex AI and tech-driven business value. • Ability to balance short-term business needs with long-term Gap’s Architecture vision. • Highly collaborative with a passion for innovation, experimentation, and user-centered design. • Comfortable driving clarity and structure in ambiguous and fast-moving environments.
...

results.title: Gap Inc. Senior Manager, Pricing Optimization job with Gap Inc. | 313658
highlights:
. Senior Manager,
...
Optimization job with Gap Inc. |
...
# Gap Inc. Senior Manager, Pricing Optimization
...
About Gap Inc.At Gap Inc., we create culture as much as we create clothes. Our ambition is to become a high-performing house of iconic American brands that shape culture. Our portfolio-Old Navy, Gap, Banana Republic, and Athleta-each brings a distinct point of view to how we show up in the world and serve our customers. Old Navy democratizes style with quality and value for all. Gap champions originality through essential pieces that celebrate individuality. Banana Republic is rooted in a spirit of discovery, creating modern pieces inspired by craftsmanship and travel. Athleta champions the Power of She through confidence, strength, and movement. We're driven by a shared purpose: to bridge gaps-between people, perspectives, and possibilities-to create a better world. We're building a team that performs at a high level-people who think boldly, take ownership, and turn ideas into impact. If you're ready to learn fast and help shape what's next, you'll fit right in.About the roleGap Inc. is transforming how its brands use data and AI to make faster, more precise commercial decisions, and the Sr. Manager, Pricing Optimization plays a key role in that transformation. Pricing is one of the most direct levers a brand has to drive profitable growth - and in this role, you will be at the center of how Old Navy pulls it. The recommendations you develop and the insights you deliver will shape how the brand prices, promotes, and marks down product, with direct impact to margin performance and revenue outcomes.You will oversee the execution of pricing and promotional optimization for Old Navy, reviewing work developed by your team of analysts and ensuring every recommendation is grounded in accurate retail analytics, demand signal synthesis, and sensitivity analysis. You will be the analytical partner for Old Navy's business owners and Finance partners -interpreting outputs from pricing agents, translating pricing signals into commercially actionable guidance, and synthesizing complex data into clear insight narratives for stakeholders. This is a people manager role: you will coach and develop a team of Pricing Analytics analysts, setting a high bar for analytical rigor, AI-informed decision making, and customer-centered commercial judgment.What You'll DoExecute Pricing & Promotional Optimization
...
- Run and validate AI-scenario simulations and decision models, applying human judgment to outputs before they inform pricing actions-confirming assumptions, flagging risks, and maintaining brand and enterprise guardrails.
...
- Interpret AI-generated pricing signals and translate them into commercially grounded recommendations, ensuring the "what" and the "so what" are always clear for merchant and finance partners.
...
- Collaborate with data science and technology partners to apply AI reporting tools and pricing platforms proficiently-operating as a skilled user and quality reviewer, not a passive recipient of outputs.
...
rigor across all work product.
...
- Foster a team culture of experimentation and continuous learning: actively test new approaches, pilot AI tools, identify what works, and build on it systematically.
...
- Working knowledge of AI-scenario simulation and decision modeling tools; you can run, interpret, and quality-check AI-generated pricing scenarios, not just consume their outputs.
...
- Proficiency with AI reporting and analytics platforms; you apply these tools fluently and coach others to do the same.
...
- AI-informed decision maker: you apply AI outputs with confidence and judgment, knowing when to trust the model, when to interrogate it, and when to escalate-and you model this mindset for your team.
...
- AI-partnership mindset: you approach agentic tools and AI workflows as a collaborator-actively exploring how they augment analytical capacity while maintaining ethical oversight and brand alignment.

Our portfolio-Old Navy, Gap, Banana Republic, and Athleta-each brings a distinct point of view to how we show up in the world and serve our customers. highlights:
Gap Inc. Strategy Analyst - Contact Centers job with Gap Inc. | 313096
...
# Gap Inc. Strategy Analyst - Contact Centers
...
Employer Gap Inc. Location Groveport, Ohio, United States Closing date 12 Jul 2026
...
Function Strategy & Business Intelligence Level Manager
...
## Job Details
...
About Gap Inc.At Gap Inc., we create culture as much as we create clothes. Our ambition is to become a high-performing house of iconic American brands that shape culture. Our portfolio-Old Navy, Gap, Banana Republic, and Athleta-each brings a distinct point of view to how we show up in the world and serve our customers. Old Navy democratizes style with quality and value for all. Gap champions originality through essential pieces that celebrate individuality. Banana Republic is rooted in a spirit of discovery, creating modern pieces inspired by craftsmanship and travel. Athleta champions the Power of She through confidence, strength, and movement. We're driven by a shared purpose: to bridge gaps-between people, perspectives, and possibilities-to create a better world. We're building a team that performs at a high level-people who think boldly, take ownership, and turn ideas into impact. If you're ready to learn fast and help shape what's next, you'll fit right in.About the roleIn this role, you will work at the intersection of data and decision-making, helping define the best path forward for Gap Inc. Post Purchase teams, with a dominant focus on customer journey. Through rigorous analysis, a sharp focus on cost and experience optimization and deep partnership with business teams, you will help capture and translate complex data into clear, strategic direction. You will support the identification of operational improvement opportunities, contribute to long-range planning, and help Post Purchase Leaders walk into key stakeholder meetings fully prepared and well-armed with insight. You will do this using a modern BI stack, built on Power BI and Databricks, with a growing emphasis on AI-augmented analysis and conversational dataeasoning model capabilities.What you'll do
...
- Drive Gap Inc. Post Purchase strategy and initiatives, from identification to planning, and execution. This includes structuring ambiguous problems, developing workplans, and delivering analysis that creates operational value across the Post Purchase team. You will use Power BI and Databricks as your primary tools, building dashboards, writing SQL and Python-based analyses, and translating data into compelling executive narratives.
...
- Apply AI-assisted analysis techniques responsibly and with appropriate judgment, using LLM tooling and AI features to accelerate structured analysis while maintaining rigor, auditability, and data governance standards.
...
- Experience with a modern BI platform such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar, including data modeling and report design for multiple stakeholder audiences. 1-2 years minimum with any major BI tool; 3+ years preferred. Hands-on Power BI experience is a plus, as it is our primary reporting tool.
...
- Comfort working in Databricks or a comparable cloud data platform with strong working knowledge of SQL. 1-2 years minimum with SQL in a professional setting; 3+ years preferred. Experience with Python or PySpark for data preparation and analysis is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with AI-assisted tooling (Copilot, LLM-based analysis, or similar) and sound judgment about when and how to apply it in a way that holds up to scrutiny.

As VP of Data & AI for Customer Journey at Gap Inc., I lead teams building data, machine learning, and AI capabilities that power end-to-end customer experiences across Gap Inc.’s portfolio of brands. This spans digital, marketing, stores, loyalty, fulfillment, and post-purchase touchpoints, with a strong focus on personalization at scale. My work centers on developing data products, algorithms, and AI-driven systems that power key customer experiences, including in-house recommendation systems, loyalty experiences (reporting, personalization, and next-best action), and marketing activation (audiences and personalized engagement). Over more than a decade at Gap Inc., I have held multiple leadership roles spanning data science, analytics, and enterprise optimization across key areas of the business, building a broad perspective and deep expertise across retail science, and how data and AI drive both operational efficiency and customer growth. My mission is to translate data and AI into measurable business impact—enabling smarter decisions, accelerating growth, and delivering personalized experiences at scale across a global retail portfolio.
...
As VP of Data & AI for Customer Journey at Gap Inc., I lead the strategy and execution of data and AI capabilities across all omnichannel customer touchpoints—digital, stores, marketing, fulfillment, loyalty, and returns—spanning a portfolio of iconic brands. This includes everything from data products and reporting to traditional machine learning, generative AI, and agentic AI, with a strong focus on personalization. I focus on delivering measurable business impact by building data and AI products that drive revenue growth and enhance customer relevance. My teams consistently target $100M+ in annual value identification through these initiatives.

With over 15 years of experience in data analysis and machine learning, I am passionate about solving complex problems and delivering innovative solutions that enhance customer experience and business performance. I lead the Partner's ML team at Shipt, where I oversee the design and development of ML models and systems that power the grocery delivery platform. Previously, I was a senior machine learning engineer at Varo Bank, where I led the development of the ML platform and feature store, and built a categorization model to identify payroll direct deposits. I have also worked at Uber, Netflix, and AT&T, where I applied ML techniques to various domains, such as location disambiguation, ad click prediction, and query classification. I have authored multiple patents, publications, and technical blog posts, and have spoken at several national and international conferences on topics related to big data and ML.

highlights:
Head of Enterprise Data Science & AI (Gap, lululemon, Levi Strauss)

A transformational leader with 15+ years of building & leading Data Science, AI/ML &…

### Head of Enterprise Data Science at [Gap Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gap-inc-) (Current)
...
I built & lead multiple data teams, all of whom use applied analytics & data to empower the lululemon global business in our decision-making & growth strategies. The first is a Data Science team, focused on delivering production Data Science/ML/AI models. We are a team that researches, builds models, and then partners with ML/Data Engineering to bring the models to market. Our focus areas are Demand Forecasting, Time Series, Optimization, recommendation, imputation, NLP. Tools & capabilities include AWS (Glue, EKS, S3, Sagemaker), GCP, (Big Query), Azure, Python, SQL, Docker, Databricks. The second team is Business Analytics. We work closely with leaders across the business to partner & prioritize, while guiding my team in delivering high quality insights & products. We are an embedded analytics team, with a rich understanding of the evolving business needs, and a talent for identifying & defining KPIs. Key wins have been inventory optimization models (eg. In Stock & Service Levels), Guest/Consumer Behavior & Analytics, Product Optimization, establishing Standardized Global reporting, and innovation within visual analytics. Our tools are Power BI, Tableau, SQL, Snowflake, Python, R, Power Query, and of course, excel. My role in these teams is to lead in strategy, prioritization, org design, and team development. I manage managers, and am deeply committed to the development of my team, and the success of my partners.
...

Senior Data Scientist E-commerce Recommendations at [Gap Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gap-inc-)
...
I'm a Data Scientist with 6+ years of experience building ML-driven solutions that deliver measurable business impact—specializing in recommender systems, predictive modeling, and end-to-end pipelines. At Gap Inc., I help build proprietary e-commerce recommendation engines that outperform third-party tools and drive revenue growth. My expertise spans the full ML lifecycle: from data exploration and feature engineering to model development, deployment, and A/B testing. I’ve worked with a wide range of techniques, from classic supervised learning to deep learning architectures like transformers, and I enjoy bridging the gap between cutting-edge models and practical business applications. My focus areas include hybrid recommendation models (collaborative filtering, content-based, transformers), scalable data engineering (Python, PySpark, SQL), and A/B testing. I work cross-functionally to translate complex technical work into actionable insights. Previously, I optimized supply chain operations through advanced analytics, developing inventory and fulfillment models that reduced costs and improved customer experience. I hold a Master's in Information Management (Data Science) from the University of Washington (GPA: 3.9), with a background in genomics research. I'm passionate about personalizing digital experiences and building production-ready solutions that combine technical rigor with business value.

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...
As a Software Engineer on the Shopping Ads team, you will be an "AI First" engineer dedicated to solving complex issues using Generative AI . You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer 1 & 2 - Systems | Hirevector
highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer 1 & 2 - Systems | Hirevector
...
# Machine Learning Engineer 1 & 2 - Systems
...
EvenUp is on a mission to close the justice gap using technology and AI. They are looking for an early career Data Scientist / Machine Learning Engineer to join their AI R&D team, focusing on developing and deploying models that enhance their claims-intelligence platform. Responsibilities Model research & prototyping – Explore, implement, and benchmark ML/NLP/generative-AI methods (e.g., LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation, document understanding) Data preparation & feature engineering – Clean, annotate, and transform structured and unstructured case data; build reusable datasets and data loaders Experimentation workflow – Design experiments, run A/B tests, analyze results, and communicate findings to the wider product and engineering teams Productionization – Help integrate models into our microservices architecture; collaborate with MLOps engineers on packaging, testing, monitoring, and scaling Cross-functional collaboration – Pair with product managers, legal analysts, and software engineers to translate pain points into ML solutions and measurable product improvements Continuous learning – Stay current with research in LLMs, representation learning, and prompt engineering; share insights through internal talks and docs Skills Ph.D., M.S. or B.S. in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, Statistics, Computational Linguistics, or a closely related field Solid grounding in machine-learning fundamentals (supervised & unsupervised learning, evaluation metrics, overfitting/regularization) Hands-on experience with NLP or generative-AI techniques (e.g., transformers, embeddings, sequence-to-sequence models, LLMs) Proficiency in Python and ML/NLP libraries such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face, spaCy, or similar Familiarity with SQL and basic data-engineering concepts (ETL, versioned datasets, notebooks) Eagerness to learn from senior teammates and iterate quickly in a fast-moving startup Clear, concise communication—both written and verbal Strong analytical thinking and a bias toward shipping pragmatic, high-impact solutions Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS/GCP) Experiment-tracking tools (Weights & Biases, MLflow) Containerized deployment (Docker/Kubernetes) Benefits Choice of medical, dental, and vision insurance plans for you and your family Additional insurance coverage options for life, accident, or critical illness Flexible paid time off, sick leave, short-term and long-term disability 10 US observed holidays, and Canadian statutory holidays by province A home office stipend 401(k) for US-based employees and RRSP for Canada-based employees Paid parental leave A local in-person meet-up program Hubs in San Francisco and Toronto Company Overview EvenUp is the leading proactive AI platform for personal injury law, the first of its kind, built on the largest personal injury dataset. It was founded in 2019, and is headquartered in San Francisco, California, USA, with a workforce of 501-1000 employees. Its website is https://www.litty.ai/.
